# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: production-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

env:
  NODE_ENV: production
  CI: true

jobs:
  build_test_deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 30
    permissions:
      contents: read
      deployments: write
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci --prefer-offline --no-audit

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test -- --ci

      - name: Build artifacts
        run: npm run build

      - name: Package artifacts
        run: tar -czf build-artifacts.tar.gz dist

      - name: Deploy to production
        env:
          DEPLOY_HOST: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_HOST }}
          DEPLOY_USER: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_USER }}
          DEPLOY_KEY: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_KEY }}
          DEPLOY_PATH: ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_PATH }}
        run: |
          mkdir -p ~/.ssh
          echo "$DEPLOY_KEY" > ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa
          ssh-keyscan -H "$DEPLOY_HOST" >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts
          rsync -az --delete build-artifacts.tar.gz "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST:$DEPLOY_PATH/"
          ssh "$DEPLOY_USER@$DEPLOY_HOST" "cd $DEPLOY_PATH && tar -xzf build-artifacts.tar.gz && rm -f build-artifacts.tar.gz"
